answer: When a Cornell student withdraws or takes a leave, federal Title IV aid may have to be returned to the extent it was unearned. Cornell states that students receiving federal Title IV aid are required to return the portion of unearned federal aid if they withdraw, go on leave of absence, do not register, or otherwise fail to complete the period of enrollment for which the aid was provided. Cornell also says it does not process Title IV leaves of absence, so academic leaves are treated as withdrawals for federal financial aid purposes.
question: What can happen to federal Title IV aid when a Cornell student withdraws or takes leave?
